stories.headline: OpenAI Proposes Handing the US Government a 5% Stake in a Sovereign AI Wealth Fund
stories.key_takeaway: Sam Altman has privately pitched Trump and top Cabinet officials on having leading US AI firms each donate 5% equity to an Alaska Permanent Fund-style public wealth vehicle, with OpenAI's share alone worth about $42.6 billion.

pull_quote.text: Returns from the Fund could be distributed directly to citizens, allowing more people to participate directly in the upside of AI-driven growth, regardless of their starting wealth or access to capital.
pull_quote.role: 'Industrial Policy for the Intelligence Age' policy paper

data_points.label: Equity each AI firm would contribute

stories.article_excerpt: Sam Altman has floated one of the most unusual pitches in the short history of the AI boom: hand the United States a stake.

stories.headline: TSMC Posts Record Revenue as AI Chip Demand Rewrites Its Calendar
stories.key_takeaway: TSMC's record $13.8B June revenue and ~$39.6B second quarter show AI chip demand from Nvidia and Apple has permanently broken the company's seasonal pattern, keeping N3 and CoWoS capacity sold out through 2026.

pull_quote.text: Demand for our leading-edge nodes remains extremely tight and sold out through 2026.

stories.article_excerpt: AI demand has broken TSMC's seasonal calendar, driving the chipmaker to an all-time monthly revenue record.

stories.headline: Chai Discovery Raises $400 Million Series C as AI-Designed Antibodies Reach Big Pharma
stories.key_takeaway: Chai Discovery raised $400M at a $3.8B valuation, tripling its worth in seven months as its AI antibody-design models reached Pfizer, Novartis and Lilly.

pull_quote.text: AI drug discovery has moved from promise to deployment, and Chai's models are already unlocking progress for our partners, enabling them to design better molecules and take on challenges traditional discovery has struggled to solve.

data_points.label: Tighter antibody binding vs prior model

stories.article_excerpt: Two years after it was founded, Chai Discovery has become one of the most richly valued startups in AI drug discovery.

stories.headline: Global Startups Raised a Record $510 Billion in H1 2026 as AI Drives the Surge
stories.key_takeaway: Global startups raised a record $510B in H1 2026, but OpenAI and Anthropic alone took 43% of it, making the boom historically dependent on a handful of frontier AI labs.

pull_quote.text: The defining characteristic of venture investment in the AI boom remains its extraordinary concentration in terms of companies, industries and geography.

data_points.label: Share taken by OpenAI and Anthropic
Q2 capital going to AI companies

stories.article_excerpt: Global startups pulled in a record $510 billion in the first half of 2026, more than investors deployed across all of 2025.

stories.headline: Anthropic Hires Monzo Co-Founder Tom Blomfield in Latest Talent Grab
stories.key_takeaway: Anthropic has hired two-time British fintech founder Tom Blomfield onto its compute team as it scales infrastructure and stacks marquee talent ahead of a possible autumn IPO.

pull_quote.text: As we enter the early stages of recursive self-improvement, availability of compute becomes one of the most important issues to solve.

data_points.label: Combined peak valuation of Monzo and GoCardless
Google TPUs Anthropic committed to deploy

stories.article_excerpt: One of Britain's most decorated fintech founders is trading the venture desk for a data-center problem.

stories.headline: Anthropic Turns Claude Artifacts Into a Multiplayer Workspace With Public Sharing and Slack
stories.key_takeaway: Anthropic reframed Claude from a solo chatbot into a shared team workspace by adding public artifact links, multiplayer editing, and the ability to build artifacts via @Claude in Slack, deepening its enterprise fight with OpenAI and Google.

pull_quote.text: Instead of a private back-and-forth, Claude Tag shows up in the open.

data_points.label: Code generated by Claude in Anthropic's product teams
Anthropic enterprise adoption, ahead of OpenAI

stories.article_excerpt: Anthropic spent the first two years of the generative-AI boom selling Claude as a brilliant solo assistant.

stories.headline: China Reaches the Frontier in AI Image and Video Generation
stories.key_takeaway: In image and video generation, Chinese labs like ByteDance, Alibaba, Kuaishou and Tencent are not catching up to the West but leading the frontier, topping independent benchmarks and dominating open-weight visual AI.

pull_quote.text: Image and video generation is the one domain where China is not catching up but competing at the frontier.

data_points.label: AI images generated daily by Seedream

stories.article_excerpt: When ByteDance quietly pushed out Seedream 5.0 Pro, the release note did not read like a company playing catch-up.

stories.headline: Runway's Gen-4 Turbo Cuts AI Video Generation Time by Roughly 60%
stories.key_takeaway: Runway's Gen-4 Turbo trades top-tier fidelity for speed and cost, rendering 10-second clips in about 30 seconds to win high-volume, iteration-driven creative workflows.

pull_quote.text: It is not only a big step forward in control and quality, but also creates a new paradigm for models that can solve many video tasks at once. The future is generalizable.

stories.article_excerpt: Runway is betting that the winner of the AI video race will not be the model that renders the most beautiful frame.

stories.headline: Meta's Latest Llama 4 Fine-Tune Extends the Model Into Dozens of Low-Resource Languages
stories.key_takeaway: Meta released an open-weight Llama 4 instruction fine-tune that extends reliable coverage from 12 supported languages into dozens more low-resource languages, sharpening the multilingual-equity race against Cohere's Aya.

pull_quote.text: From the beginning, we doubled the number of languages that were covered by generative AI.

stories.article_excerpt: When Meta shipped Llama 4, the open-weight model was pretrained across roughly 200 languages but reliable in far fewer.

stories.headline: Google's Gemini 3.5 Pro Is Expected July 17 With a 2-Million-Token Context Window
stories.key_takeaway: Google's Gemini 3.5 Pro is expected to launch July 17 with a reported 2-million-token context window and an Ultra-tier Deep Think mode, colliding with Xi Jinping's appearance at Shanghai's World AI Conference.

pull_quote.text: When a frontier launch and a head-of-state AI summit land on the same day, on opposite sides of the planet, that is the whole 2026 story in a single calendar square.
pull_quote.role: Chief Education Officer, Build Fast with AI

data_points.label: Reported price per M input tokens
Expected launch, same as WAIC opening

stories.article_excerpt: Google's most anticipated model of the summer is reportedly three days out, and the calendar could hardly be more loaded.

stories.headline: An ICML 2026 Method Lets Small Models Reason Like Ones Three Times Their Size
stories.key_takeaway: A training method called selective activation sparsity, surfaced at ICML 2026, lets small models match the reasoning performance of models roughly three times their size by activating only the most task-relevant parameters.

pull_quote.text: On reasoning benchmarks, models trained with this method performed comparably to models three times their size.

stories.article_excerpt: For most of the last five years, the reliable way to make an AI model reason better has been to make it bigger.

stories.headline: Vector Institute Brings 73 Papers to ICML 2026, Spanning Reasoning, Video, and Agents
stories.key_takeaway: Toronto's Vector Institute brought a record 73 papers and 11 spotlights to ICML 2026 in Seoul, mapping the AI frontier from video attribution to agentic RL and signaling the payoff of Canada's public AI-research bet.

pull_quote.text: We believe that this rigor is the cornerstone of thoughtful and reproducible research that can be built upon.

data_points.label: Human-preference win rate after Motive curation

stories.article_excerpt: When the International Conference on Machine Learning opened in Seoul on July 6, one delegation stood out for its scale.

stories.headline: Auditing Multi-Agent Reasoning Trees Beats Majority Vote and LLM-as-Judge
stories.key_takeaway: AgentAuditor replaces majority voting in multi-agent LLM systems with an audit of the agents' merged reasoning tree, beating majority vote by up to 5% and LLM-as-judge by up to 3% in absolute accuracy across five settings.

pull_quote.text: This heuristic discards the evidential structure of reasoning traces and is brittle under the confabulation consensus, where agents share correlated biases and converge on the same incorrect rationale.
pull_quote.role: Paper authors, 'Auditing Multi-Agent LLM Reasoning Trees'

stories.article_excerpt: When several AI agents talk out a hard problem and then take a vote, the crowd is supposed to be wiser than any member.

stories.headline: Twelve Montefiore Nurses Laid Off and Replaced by AI Software, Union Says
stories.key_takeaway: Montefiore is moving to replace 12 licensed utilization-review nurses across three Bronx campuses with Datavant AI software, which NYSNA says violates AI-protection contract language won after a 41-day strike and endangers patients.

pull_quote.text: Stop the layoffs, keep a licensed nurse on the final review, use AI to support us instead of replacing us, and sit down with the nurses who actually do this work.

data_points.label: Days of the strike that won the contract
Years of service for one affected nurse

stories.article_excerpt: Marilyn Shuler has spent 39 years as a nurse at Montefiore Medical Center in the Bronx, now among 12 facing layoff.

stories.headline: The UN Launches Its First Global Dialogue on AI Governance Amid 'Catastrophic Harm' Warnings
stories.key_takeaway: The UN convened all 193 member states for its first multilateral AI governance forum, but a stark scientific warning of possible 'catastrophic harm' collided with a US-China split and the absence of any binding enforcement power.

pull_quote.text: Artificial intelligence is advancing at runaway speed. An experiment is being run on our own societies, without a plan, and without consent.

data_points.label: UN member states at the table

stories.article_excerpt: For the first time in the organization's eight decades, all 193 UN member states sat down to govern AI together.
